topic_1_tauri_react_project_structure.title: Tauri 2.0 + React Recommended Project Structure and Configuration

root.package.json: Frontend dependencies, scripts (dev, build, tauri dev, tauri build)
root.tsconfig.json: TypeScript root config with path aliases

src-tauri/.Cargo.toml: Rust dependencies including tauri, tauri-plugin-shell, etc.

capabilities/.default.json: Default capability with permissions for all windows

src/.lib.rs: Shared library code (mobile entry point), plugin registration
src/.commands/: Tauri command modules (Rust functions callable from JS)

state_management_recommendation.approach: Three-layer state management (community best practice from dannysmith/tauri-template)

layers.component_state: React useState/useReducer for local UI state
layers.global_ui_state: Zustand for cross-component shared state (sidebar open, active view, theme)
layers.server_state: TanStack Query for async data fetching, caching, and sync with backend

state_management_recommendation.rationale: Zustand is lightweight (~1KB), has no providers/context needed, integrates well with React 19, and ReactFlow internally uses Zustand for its own store.

type_safe_bridge.recommendation: tauri-specta for auto-generating TypeScript types from Rust commands
type_safe_bridge.benefit: Full autocomplete and compile-time checking for Tauri IPC calls
type_safe_bridge.alternative: Manual type definitions in src/types/ if tauri-specta adds too much complexity initially

topic_2_reactflow_custom_nodes_edges.title: ReactFlow Custom Nodes/Edges Best Practices for Knowledge Graph Whiteboard

custom_node_implementation.pattern: Define custom node components as separate files, register via nodeTypes object

type_safety.define_node_data_type: type KnowledgeNodeData = { label: string; mastery: number; status: 'not_started' | 'learning' | 'mastered'; lastReview?: Date; }
type_safety.type_guard: function isKnowledgeNode(node: Node): node is KnowledgeNode { return node.type === 'knowledge'; }

example_custom_node.description: A knowledge concept node with mastery indicator, handles for connections
key_elements: Handle components for source/target connections (Position.Top, Position.Bottom, Position.Left, Position.Right)
Conditional styling based on mastery level
example_custom_node.code_pattern:
const KnowledgeNode = memo(({ data, selected }: NodeProps) => { return (
{data.label}
); }); KnowledgeNode.displayName = 'KnowledgeNode';

nodeTypes_registration.pattern: Define nodeTypes OUTSIDE the component (or useMemo) to prevent re-creation on every render
nodeTypes_registration.code: const nodeTypes = { knowledge: KnowledgeNode, topic: TopicNode, resource: ResourceNode }; // MUST be stable reference

custom_edge_implementation.pattern: Custom edges can render any React component along the edge path

recommended_for_knowledge_graph.reason: Floating edges connect to node borders dynamically, not fixed handle positions - better for organic graph layouts
recommended_for_knowledge_graph.label_support: EdgeLabelRenderer for rendering interactive labels on edges (relationship type, strength)

custom_edge_implementation.edgeTypes_registration: const edgeTypes = { relationship: RelationshipEdge }; // MUST be stable reference outside component

memoize_components.rule: ALL custom node and edge components MUST use React.memo()
memoize_components.reason: ReactFlow triggers frequent re-renders on node drag/viewport change

memoize_callbacks.rule: ALL event handler functions passed to <ReactFlow> MUST use useCallback()

stable_type_references.rule: nodeTypes and edgeTypes objects MUST be defined outside the component or wrapped in useMemo
stable_type_references.anti_pattern: Defining nodeTypes inside render function causes all nodes to unmount/remount every render

viewport_culling.benefit: Only renders nodes/edges visible in the viewport - major perf gain for 500+ nodes

css_optimization.rule: Avoid complex CSS (animations, shadows, gradients) on node components when dealing with 200+ nodes
css_optimization.alternative: Use simple borders and background colors; add effects only on hover/selection

recommended_libraries.name: elkjs
recommended_libraries.use_case: Hierarchical/layered layouts for directed knowledge graphs
recommended_libraries.integration: Calculate positions with ELK, apply to ReactFlow nodes

recommended_libraries.name: dagre
recommended_libraries.use_case: Simpler DAG layouts, lighter weight than ELK
recommended_libraries.integration: Well-documented ReactFlow example in official docs

layout_integration.manual_layout: Allow users to manually drag nodes and persist positions - essential for a whiteboard experience

store_integration.reactflow_internal_store: ReactFlow uses Zustand internally - accessible via useStore() hook

external_store_pattern.recommendation: Use a separate Zustand store for app-level graph state, sync with ReactFlow via controlled mode
external_store_pattern.controlled_mode: Pass nodes/edges as props + onNodesChange/onEdgesChange handlers for full control
external_store_pattern.type_safe_store: type GraphStore = { nodes: KnowledgeNode[]; edges: Edge[]; addNode: (node: KnowledgeNode) => void; updateNodeMastery: (id: string, mastery: number) => void; }

topic_3_shadcn_tailwind_configuration.title: shadcn/ui + TailwindCSS in Tauri Recommended Configuration

installation_steps.step_1: Create Vite+React project (already done via create-tauri-app)

step_2_tailwind.note: For Tailwind v4, use CSS-first config instead of tailwind.config.ts

step_3_path_aliases.tsconfig_app_json: MUST also include the same paths config (Vite reads this file)
step_3_path_aliases.vite_config: Install vite-tsconfig-paths plugin OR manually configure resolve.alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src') }

installation_steps.step_5_add_components: npx shadcn@latest add button card dialog dropdown-menu input etc.

globals_css_structure.description: For Tailwind v4, the CSS file IS the config
globals_css_structure.theme_inline: Map CSS variables to Tailwind color tokens via @theme inline { --color-background: var(--background); ... }
globals_css_structure.root_variables: Define :root { } and .dark { } with oklch color values
globals_css_structure.base_layer: @layer base { * { @apply border-border outline-ring/50; } body { @apply bg-background text-foreground; } }

theme_provider.description: shadcn provides a ThemeProvider component using React Context
features: Uses matchMedia for system theme detection

dark_mode_support.mode_toggle: shadcn ModeToggle component with DropdownMenu for switching themes

topic_4_tauri_shell_plugin_docker.title: Tauri Shell Plugin for Managing Docker Compose

execute_and_wait.description: Run docker-compose command and wait for completion

spawn_with_streaming.description: Start docker-compose and stream logs in real-time

check_docker_status.description: Check if Docker daemon is running

important_notes: On some systems, you may need to use the full path to docker-compose (e.g., /usr/local/bin/docker-compose) instead of just 'docker-compose'
The validator regex in args prevents command injection by restricting argument patterns
Each command name (docker-compose-up, etc.) must exactly match what you pass to Command.create()
Consider using 'docker compose' (v2 subcommand) instead of 'docker-compose' for newer Docker Desktop versions
For Windows, the cmd may need to be 'docker-compose.exe' or the full path

cross_platform_handling.recommendation: Create a Rust command that detects the platform and returns the correct docker path
cross_platform_handling.pattern: Tauri command in Rust determines OS -> returns appropriate docker binary path -> JS uses that path
cross_platform_handling.alternative: Bundle docker-compose.yml path relative to app data dir using Tauri's path plugin

topic_5_csp_configuration.title: Tauri tauri.conf.json CSP Configuration Recommended Values
principles: Start strict, loosen only as needed
Tauri auto-appends nonces/hashes for bundled code at compile time
Only worry about CSP for your app-specific external resources
Avoid 'unsafe-inline' for scripts; use nonces/hashes instead
'unsafe-inline' for styles is sometimes necessary (inline Tailwind, ReactFlow)

explanation_per_directive.default-src: Fallback for all resource types. 'self' + Tauri custom protocols
explanation_per_directive.script-src: 'self' only - Tauri auto-adds nonces for inline scripts at build time
explanation_per_directive.style-src: 'unsafe-inline' needed for Tailwind's runtime styles and ReactFlow's inline positioning
explanation_per_directive.img-src: blob: and data: needed for canvas screenshots, node thumbnails
explanation_per_directive.connect-src: ipc: for Tauri IPC, localhost:* for local backend API calls and WebSocket connections
explanation_per_directive.worker-src: blob: needed if using Web Workers for heavy computation

when_to_adjust.external_fonts: Add https://fonts.gstatic.com to font-src and https://fonts.googleapis.com to style-src
when_to_adjust.external_images: Add specific CDN domains to img-src
when_to_adjust.wasm: Add 'wasm-unsafe-eval' to script-src if using WebAssembly

topic_6_react19_vite_tauri_build.title: React 19 + Vite + Tauri Build Configuration Best Practices

dependency_optimization.rationale: Pre-bundle large dependencies for faster dev server startup

build_targets.es2021_chrome100_safari14: Tauri WebView minimum targets (WebView2 on Windows = Chromium, WebKit on macOS)
build_targets.no_ssr: Tauri is client-only, no SSR considerations needed

flavors.mocha: Darkest mode (highest contrast, recommended default dark)

integration_approach_tailwind_v4.flavor_switching: Apply flavor class to HTML element: <html class='mocha'> or <html class='latte'>

bridging_catppuccin_with_shadcn.approach: Map Catppuccin colors to shadcn CSS variables using oklch conversion
bridging_catppuccin_with_shadcn.description: shadcn uses oklch-based CSS variables (--background, --foreground, --primary, etc.). Map Catppuccin flavor colors to these variables.

implementation_options.option: A: Manual oklch mapping in globals.css
implementation_options.description: Manually convert Catppuccin hex values to oklch and set in :root/.dark CSS variables
implementation_options.pros: Full control, no extra dependencies, works with shadcn as-is
implementation_options.cons: Manual conversion work for all 4 flavors x ~25 variables

implementation_options.option: B: Use @catppuccin/tailwindcss plugin + CSS variable bridge
implementation_options.description: Use the Catppuccin plugin for direct Catppuccin class usage, plus a bridge CSS file that aliases ctp variables to shadcn variables
implementation_options.pros: Both systems work - direct ctp- classes AND shadcn components pick up theme
implementation_options.cons: Two theming systems running in parallel, potential confusion

implementation_options.description: Use tweakcn.com or gradient.page theme generator to create a Catppuccin-inspired shadcn theme
implementation_options.pros: Simplest, single theming system, all shadcn components themed correctly
implementation_options.cons: May not be pixel-perfect Catppuccin, harder to switch between all 4 flavors

bridging_catppuccin_with_shadcn.recommendation: Option A (manual oklch mapping) for the initial MVP. It provides exact Catppuccin colors in shadcn's native system with no extra dependencies. Support Latte (light) and Mocha (dark) first, add Frappe/Macchiato later.

catppuccin_color_reference.note: Key color names used across all flavors

theme_switcher.description: Extend shadcn ThemeProvider to support 4 Catppuccin flavors
theme_switcher.mechanism: Toggle CSS class on <html> element: 'latte' (light) vs 'mocha' (dark), each having their own CSS variable block

dev_workflow.dev_command: npm run tauri dev (starts both Vite dev server and Tauri window)
dev_workflow.build_command: npm run tauri build (produces native binary with bundled frontend)
dev_workflow.hot_reload: Vite HMR works in Tauri WebView - instant updates during development

testing_strategy.unit_tests: Vitest for React component and store tests
testing_strategy.e2e_tests: Tauri has built-in WebDriver support, or use Playwright
testing_strategy.tauri_command_mocking: Mock Tauri IPC in tests using @tauri-apps/api/mocks

confidence_levels.topic_1_project_structure: HIGH - Verified via official Tauri docs + dannysmith/tauri-template production template
confidence_levels.topic_2_reactflow: HIGH - Verified via official ReactFlow (@xyflow/react) docs with extensive code examples
confidence_levels.topic_3_shadcn_tailwind: HIGH - Verified via official shadcn/ui docs with Vite-specific installation guide and CSS variable system
confidence_levels.topic_4_shell_plugin_docker: MEDIUM-HIGH - Shell plugin API well-documented, but docker-compose specific scoped permissions are our design (follows Tauri security patterns)
confidence_levels.topic_5_csp: HIGH - Verified via official Tauri security documentation
confidence_levels.topic_6_build_config: HIGH - Verified via official Tauri Vite guide + community best practices
confidence_levels.topic_7_catppuccin: MEDIUM-HIGH - @catppuccin/tailwindcss is official and well-maintained, but bridging with shadcn oklch variables requires manual mapping work

key_risks: Catppuccin-to-shadcn color bridging requires careful oklch conversion for all 4 flavors (~100 variable mappings total)
Docker binary paths differ across OS (Windows/macOS/Linux) - needs platform detection in Tauri commands
ReactFlow performance with 500+ nodes needs careful memoization discipline from day one
Tailwind v4 CSS-first config is relatively new - some shadcn component installation may need workarounds
recommended_bootstrap_sequence: 2. Configure vite.config.ts with SWC + path aliases
4. Run npx shadcn@latest init + add core components
5. Create Catppuccin oklch variable mappings in globals.css (Latte + Mocha)
7. Add tauri-plugin-shell + configure Docker Compose capabilities
8. Set up Zustand stores for graph state and app state
